narrator_text:
The shepherds drop out of the picture in the first hundred yards. Then the goat-track. Then the last sympathy-bell from the third farmstead, fading to nothing as the deep pine swallows sound.

The scent stays half a degree off true south the whole night. Banked-coal warmth, fixed, patient. Shen walks under it like a needle under a north star. Crickets. A vixen barking at the moon. A chimney glow seven miles east where a watchpost burns its lantern through full dark. None of it pulls his head around.

By the second hour after midnight the pine starts climbing. By the fourth, granite. By dawn — the broken edge where the foothills surrender to the Dragonspine pass proper. Cold wind off the snowline.

Then the air changes.

Pressure drop. A weight on the chest-glyph that has nothing to do with scent. Old wind. *Mountain* wind. Cold the way only one wind on this continent is cold.

A shadow crosses the dawn light — too large for cloud, too low for sun.

She lands on the path twenty yards ahead. Fifty feet of black scale and folded wings. Green eyes that match his own.

**"Shen."**

One word. The path goes nowhere now.
options: Lower the head, present the stolen scale, acknowledge the call.
Pivot and sprint downhill into the pass — gamble on her wing-radius being too tight for the steep cuts.
Set your feet and wait — whatever she does first, answer it second.
